case_memory.chief_complaint: ___ yo woman with past medical history of endometrial
current_turn_information: I’ve been constipated for 12 days, and then for several days now I’ve had diarrhea that looks like mucus. I don’t have blood in my stool or in the toilet bowl.
I’ve also been having cramping pain in my lower abdomen for about a month, which gets worse when I strain to have a bowel movement — even after taking Miralax and Mag Citrate.

A few days ago, when I was straining, I got lightheaded. I went back to my bedroom and was getting out of the bathroom when I suddenly felt dizzy, lost consciousness, and came to as I felt like my ankle was breaking.
I was on the floor for about an hour before my husband found me.
In the ER, I had a right ankle fracture, and orthopedic surgery put a splint on it.
I’m still having abdominal discomfort now, but I’m breathing fine on room air.
I don’t have fever, chills, headache, or any other symptoms like nausea, vomiting, or pain with urination.

Social history: family history of asthma/depression (mother), heart murmur (sister). Chief complaint: ___ yo woman with past medical history of endometrial. HPI: constipation x12 days, diarrhea with mucus, cramping lower abdominal pain x1 month, lightheadedness during straining, syncope with ankle fracture, splint applied. Final result: fracture of lateral malleolus, closed. Knowledge: Basophilia definition, causes, diagnosis.
